meaning: A piece or segment of a whole; a role played by an actor.
example: This is the most difficult part of the job.
example2: She played the part of Juliet in the school play.
example3: Auto parts are sold in that store.
Explanation: Part is an extremely common noun and verb. As a noun, it means a portion, component, or division of something larger. It can be physical (car part) or abstract (part of the problem). It also means a role in a performance or an area (these parts of town). As a verb, it means to separate or divide ('part your hair', 'we parted ways'). The word is fundamental to describing composition, participation, and division. Phrases like 'for the most part', 'play a part', and 'take part' are ubiquitous in English.
usageTip: Use 'part of' (usually without 'a') before a non-countable noun or a singular countable noun (part of the team, part of the water).
commonMistake: Saying 'a part of' when 'part of' is often sufficient and more idiomatic.

meaning: A piece or section of a whole, or to separate things
example: The keyboard is an essential part of the computer setup.
example2: This is the most difficult part of the project.
example3: They had to part the two servers to fix the cooling issue.
Explanation: The word 'part' is very common. As a noun, it refers to one of the pieces that make up a system (like hardware parts or parts of a code function). As a verb, it means to separate. In IT, we often talk about 'parting' data into chunks or 'partitioning' a hard drive. It is a fundamental word used in both general and technical English to describe components and structure.
usageTip: Use 'part' to describe a single piece of a larger machine or plan.
commonMistake: Using 'part' when 'component' might sound more professional in a technical report.
